2606.10890.title: Optimal Post-Training Quantization Scales and Where to Find Them
2606.10890.paper_summary: Post-training quantization (PTQ) compresses large language models by mapping weights to low-bit representations. The scaling factor that defines the quantization grid is typically chosen using simple, data-free heuristics. In this work, we present PiSO (Piecewise Scale Optimization), an algorithm that leverages calibration data to compute the optimal channel-wise weight scales exactly and efficiently under round-to-nearest quantization. PiSO partitions the scale search space into finitely many intervals on which the objective admits a closed-form minimizer. We extend PiSO to group-wise quantization via principled heuristics and propose effective strategies for interleaving scale optimization with error correction. Experiments on Llama and Qwen models across multiple model sizes and target weight bit-widths demonstrate consistent improvements in perplexity and downstream zero-shot accuracy, both standalone and combined with error correction. In particular, we observe increased benefits as the target bit-width narrows and quantization becomes more challenging.
2606.10890.paper_summary_zh: 後訓練量化 (PTQ) 通過將權重映射到低位表示來壓縮大型語言模型。定義量化網格的縮放因子通常使用簡單的無數據啟發式方法來選擇。在這項工作中，我們提出了 PiSO（分段縮放優化），這是一種利用校準數據來精確且高效地計算最佳通道權重縮放的算法，適用於四捨五入量化。PiSO 將縮放搜索空間劃分為有限多個區間，在這些區間上目標函數允許閉合形式的最小化。我們通過原則性啟發式方法將 PiSO 擴展到組別量化，並提出有效的策略來交錯縮放優化與錯誤修正。在多個模型大小和目標權重位寬的 Llama 和 Qwen 模型上的實驗顯示，在困惑度和下游零-shot 準確性方面都有一致的改進，無論是單獨使用還是與錯誤修正結合使用。特別是，當目標位寬縮小且量化變得更加具挑戰性時，我們觀察到益處增加。
2606.10890.author: Juan Amboage et.al.
2606.10890.authors: Juan Amboage, Pablo Monteagudo-Lago, Ian Colbert, Giuseppe Franco, Nicholas Fraser
